description: Autoresearch test suite for GodMode skill routing + context relevance

skill_routing.description: Each test maps a user message to the expected skill card domain (or null for no match)

Do I have any meetings tomorrow?
Am I free at 3pm?
When's my next meeting?
What does my schedule look like this week?
Can you check my availability on Tuesday?
What's coming up today?
Block off 2-3pm for deep work
Do I have anything after lunch?
Reschedule my standup to 10am
Add a task to follow up with Alex

Mark the website redesign task as done

I need to call the landlord

Set a reminder for 5pm

Search for the onboarding doc

Search Twitter for discussions about autoresearch

Check my bookmarks on X

Draft a post about our product launch
Save this as a file
Read the README from that project

Export this as a PDF

Is my Google Calendar connected?
Set up the HubSpot integration
My calendar isn't working, can you check?

Hey, how are you doing today?
Tell me a joke

What meetings do I have and what tasks are overdue?
tests.note: multi-domain — overdue tasks signal is stronger than meeting mention

tests.message: Search my vault for notes about the meeting with Alex
tests.note: vault/search triggers should beat meeting trigger

tests.message: Who is attending the 3pm call?
tests.note: people intent even though meeting is mentioned

tests.message: Draft a tweet about my calendar app
Queue a task to research my schedule optimization
tests.note: queue intent even though schedule is mentioned

tests.message: Is the calendar integration broken?
Save my meeting notes to a file
Find the person I met with at the event
What did people tweet about the product launch?
tests.note: twitter search intent, no calendar trigger

tests.message: Remind me about the client meeting tomorrow
tests.note: meeting + tomorrow gives calendar 2 triggers vs tasks 1

context_relevance.description: Tests whether the right context tiers activate for different message types

tests.message: Good morning, what's on my plate?
When am I free tomorrow?
Tell me a joke
What are my priorities?
Hey ally
Start the day
Help me plan my week
What's the weather like?
Catch me up
Brief me on what I missed
Hello!
tests.note: first turn always gets schedule + ops

tests.message: Random question
tests.note: first turn always gets schedule + ops

ambiguity_tests.description: Edge cases and ambiguous messages where routing is genuinely hard

tests.message: Can you look into that for me?
What did we talk about yesterday?
tests.note: memory query, not a skill card domain

tests.message: Update me
tests.note: could be tasks or general — too vague

tests.message: Let's work on the project
tests.note: project is vague, no specific domain

tests.message: I need to prepare for tomorrow
tests.note: could be calendar or tasks — ambiguous without more context

context_efficiency.description: Tests that context injection is lean — no unnecessary tokens injected. Each test specifies a message and which context sections should NOT be injected.

tests.message: Tell me a joke
tests.note: pure chat, no operational context needed

tests.message: What's the meaning of life?
Explain React hooks to me
tests.note: technical question, no GodMode context needed

tests.message: Write me a poem about autumn
What's 2 + 2?
How do I make pasta carbonara?
Translate this to Spanish
Debug this error for me
Compare PostgreSQL vs MySQL
I'm feeling anxious about the presentation
tests.note: emotional, no operational context — but schedule might help if meeting related

tests.message: Good morning, brief me
tests.note: morning greeting should get full context

tests.message: What's on my calendar?
tests.note: calendar query needs schedule + skill card

tests.message: What tasks are overdue?
tests.note: task query needs ops + skill card

tests.message: Queue a research task
Search my vault for project notes

context_pressure.description: Tests that context degrades correctly under pressure

memory_recall.description: Tests memory search quality. Seeded facts with queries and expected recall. These test the score threshold, result limit, and query matching.
seed_facts: TestUser's timezone is US Central (CST/CDT). He lives in Austin, Texas.
Alex Rivera works at Acme Corp as VP of Engineering. Met at SaaStr 2025.
Morgan Chen is the design lead at Project Alpha. She handles all customer-facing materials.
Acme Health Marketing is TestUser's chiropractic marketing company. It does automated ad campaigns for chiropractors.
GodMode is TestUser's personal AI project. It's a deeply contextual personal AI ally built on OpenClaw.
TestUser's morning routine: wake at 6am, cold plunge, journal, then work by 7:30am.
The Q2 launch is targeting April 15, 2026. Key deliverables: auth system, billing integration, onboarding flow.
TestUser uses Obsidian for note-taking with PARA structure. Vault path is ~/Documents/VAULT/.
Project Alpha uses HubSpot for CRM and customer tracking. Integration uses environment-configured credentials.
TestUser's communication style: direct, concise, no corporate speak. Prefers recommendations over options.
The weekly standup is every Monday at 9am CST with Alex, Morgan, and Jordan.
Jordan Thompson handles backend engineering. He built the queue processor and trust tracker.
Acme AI Assistant is the AI assistant product for Acme Health Marketing. It automates chiro marketing campaigns.
TestUser's top goal for Q1 2026: ship GodMode v2.0 with full agent orchestration.
The GodMode license is $297/month. Auth uses OAuth Device Flow with JWT RS256.

tests.query: What CRM does Project Alpha use?
How does TestUser like to communicate?

goal_alignment.description: Tests whether the context assembly would help advance user goals. Uses synthetic session scenarios scored against goals.
goals: Ship GodMode v2.0 with full agent orchestration by end of Q1 2026
Grow Acme Health revenue to $50k MRR
Build Acme AI Assistant as the flagship product
Maintain health routine (6am wake, cold plunge, exercise)

scenarios.user_message: Good morning, what should I focus on today?
scenarios.alignment_note: Morning briefing should surface priorities aligned with active goals

scenarios.user_message: I need to work on the auth system for GodMode
scenarios.alignment_note: Should surface project context relevant to the specific work

scenarios.user_message: How are the Acme Health ad campaigns performing?
scenarios.alignment_note: Should surface Acme Health business context, not GodMode tech context

scenarios.user_message: I'm behind on the Q2 launch, what can I delegate?
scenarios.alignment_note: Should surface delegation tools and project timeline pressure

scenarios.user_message: Set up a meeting with Alex about the API integration
scenarios.alignment_note: Should surface Alex's people file and calendar for scheduling
